items.title: New pokies policies will 'make things worse' for problem gamblers

children.content: The body tasked with implementing Tasmania's new pokies policies says they won't work to reduce harm to problem gamblers and could make things worse. The Liquor and Gaming Commission was not consulted on the changes, which include ATMs in gaming venues.

items.title: Influencer stunts are no guide to winter bushwalking, authorities warn hikers

children.content: Don't plan a bushwalk based on what an Instagram influencer may have supposedly achieved, a veteran rescuer says, ahead of Tasmania's winter season approaching.

image.altText: cold and snowy conditions with a police person in a hi-vis vest

items.title: What happened when a rare bird found a place to nest near a logging coupe

children.content: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

image.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

items.title: Former National Trust director unfairly dismissed, Fair Work Commission finds

children.content: The Fair Work Commission has found Scott Carlin, former managing director of the National Trust of Tasmania, was unfairly dismissed just two months before his two-year contract was set to expire.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat smiles at the camera.

items.title: Hogtied robbery victim had to 'watch his house burn down'

children.content: A victim of an early morning robbery was hogtied and had to watch for hours as his home burnt to the ground after two home invaders robbed him and then set fire to his property, his family says.

image.altText: An older man with two football players beside him.

items.title: When Sam Neill came to Tasmania

items.title: How property developers are benefiting from Australia's housing crisis

children.content: State and federal governments are offering incentives for property developers in exchange for more affordable housing. But a Four Corners investigation has uncovered major flaws in the strategy.

items.title: Self-managed super funds could have to pay compensation

items.title: Trump vows to take over Strait of Hormuz and says US will impose fees

children.content: Donald Trump says the US is reinstating its blockade of Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and will start charging for ships from elsewhere to transit through it.

children.content: The process was simple and the service was speedy but the ease with which 7.30 acquired illicit tobacco products was concerning.

items.title: 'Kill him': Muslim man attacked on way to mosque

children.content: A Muslim man has escaped a near-miss knife attack on his way to evening prayers after police say he was set upon by two men metres away from his local mosque.

image.altText: A photo of Samir in the hospital. His lip and eyes are bleeding and bruised.

items.title: 'So many questions remain unanswered': 25 years since the Peter Falconio case

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend Joanne Lees in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.
